changes: Abstract: Removed "emerges through in-situ reconstruction", added "engineered through explicit bistable drift function"
Section 3.4: Added explicit note that multistability arises from designed drift function, not microscopic rules
Section 4.1: Added distinction between microscopic (stochastic fragmentation) vs designed (hard bistability) mechanisms
Section 4.3: Added limitation noting that hard bistability is designed, not emergent
Section 5: Changed "naturally give rise" to "demonstrate that dimension and coupling can be engineered"
Conclusion: Updated key findings to reflect "designed multistability" and "coupling controls dimensionality"

complexity_reconstruction.mechanism: 96.4% of core structures are newly generated

multistability.designed_drift: F(M) = α·M·(1-M)·(M-a) produces hard bistability (M=0 and M=1)

theoretical_framework.key_insight: Two distinct regimes: (1) microscopic rules → complexity reconstruction, (2) designed drift → multistability
